name: New Markets Tax Credit (NMTC)
authorizing_statute: IRC s.45D; made permanent by OBBBA (P.L.119-21, 2025)
pass_through_path: Treasury CDFI Fund -> Community Development Entity (CDE) -> Qualified Active Low-Income Community Business
eligible_uses: Equity investment in low-income community businesses/real estate; 39% credit over 7 yrs
match_cost_share: None (private capital; tax-exempt sponsor uses leverage loan)
status_2026: Made PERMANENT under OBBBA (was set to expire 12/31/2025); ~$5B annual allocation authority

name: Carbon Oxide Sequestration Credit (Section 45Q)
authorizing_statute: IRC s.45Q; amended by IRA (2022) and OBBBA (2025)
pass_through_path: Direct to taxpayer; tax-exempt entity via elective pay s.6417 or transfer s.6418
eligible_uses: Carbon capture/utilization/geologic storage equipment; credit per metric ton CO2
status_2026: PRESERVED & STRENGTHENED by OBBBA: EOR/utilization rate raised to $85/ton (parity with storage) for equipment placed in service after 7/4/2025; FEOC limits apply to tax years beginning after enactment (SFEs ineligible; FIEs +2 yrs); transfers barred to SFEs

name: Elective (Direct) Pay
pass_through_path: Direct to applicable (tax-exempt/governmental) entity as a payment
eligible_uses: Lets tax-exempt clients monetize 45Q/48E/45Y/etc. as a cash refund rather than a credit
status_2026: RETAINED for nonprofits/state-local govts under OBBBA; domestic-content phase-downs and FEOC limits tighten from 2026; closing windows on wind/solar (48E/45Y) underlying credits

name: Transferability of Credits
pass_through_path: Sale of credit to unrelated taxpayer for cash
eligible_uses: Monetize credits a tax-exempt entity cannot use directly; alternative/complement to elective pay
status_2026: RETAINED under OBBBA; 45Q credits may NOT be transferred to Specified Foreign Entities; FEOC narrows transferee pool from 2026

name: Low-Income Housing Tax Credit (LIHTC)
eligible_uses: Acquisition/rehab/new construction of affordable rental housing
status_2026: EXPANDED by OBBBA: 9% state ceiling permanently +12%; 4% bond-financing threshold permanently lowered 50%->25% (more 4% deals viable) for buildings placed in service after 12/31/2025

name: Historic Rehabilitation Tax Credit (HTC)
pass_through_path: Direct to owner; ratably over 5 yrs
eligible_uses: 20% credit for certified rehab of historic buildings
status_2026: UNCHANGED at federal level by OBBBA (HTC Growth & Opportunity Act provisions NOT included); remains 20% / 5-yr

name: Clean Electricity ITC / PTC (48E / 45Y)
authorizing_statute: IRC s.48E, s.45Y; curtailed by OBBBA
eligible_uses: Investment/production credit for clean electricity facilities
status_2026: CLOSING WINDOW: OBBBA terminates wind/solar 48E/45Y for facilities placed in service after 12/31/2027; full credit only if construction begins before 7/4/2026; FEOC material-assistance (MACR) applies to construction starting 2026 (40% facilities / 55% storage threshold). IRS Notice 2026-15 (2/12/2026) interim guidance

name: Clean Water State Revolving Fund (CWSRF)
authorizing_statute: Clean Water Act Title VI (s.601 et seq.); IIJA supplemental
formula_or_competitive: Formula (cap grant to states) then state RLF
eligible_uses: Wastewater, stormwater, nonpoint source, water-quality portions of brownfield/site cleanup
match_cost_share: State match 20% on cap grant; subsidy varies (principal forgiveness for disadvantaged)
period_of_performance: Project life (loan term up to 30-40 yrs)
status_2026: FUNDED FY2026: P.L.119-74 provides ~$3.04B EPA water infrastructure (= FY2025 level); EPA announced ~$7.2B total CWSRF+DWSRF allotments (annual approp + IIJA)

name: Drinking Water State Revolving Fund (DWSRF)
authorizing_statute: Safe Drinking Water Act s.1452; IIJA supplemental
eligible_uses: Drinking water infrastructure, lead service line replacement, treatment, source water
match_cost_share: State match 20%; principal forgiveness for disadvantaged communities
status_2026: FUNDED FY2026 (see CWSRF; combined ~$7.2B allotments announced)

name: Brownfields Assessment, RLF & Cleanup Grants
eligible_uses: Brownfield site assessment, cleanup, revolving loan funds, area-wide planning
match_cost_share: 20% cost share on cleanup (may be hardship-waived)
status_2026: Recurring annual competitive program; pairs with CWSRF for water-quality remediation; verify current NOFO cycle on grants.gov

name: USDA Rural Water & Waste Disposal (RD)
authorizing_statute: Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act s.306
eligible_uses: Water/wastewater systems in rural areas; loans + grants
match_cost_share: Grant share varies by median household income
status_2026: Standing RD program; verify FY2026 allocation and rural eligibility

name: FEMA Building Resilient Infrastructure & Communities (BRIC)
authorizing_statute: Stafford Act s.203 (as amended by DRRA 2018 s.1234)
entitlement_or_discretionary: Discretionary (funded from Disaster Relief Fund set-aside)
pass_through_path: FEMA -> state emergency mgmt agency -> local subapplicant
eligible_uses: Pre-disaster hazard mitigation: infrastructure hardening, codes, mitigation planning
status_2026: RESTORED: terminated 4/4/2025; court permanently enjoined termination 12/11/2025; enforcement order 3/6/2026; FEMA issued joint FY2024-2025 BRIC NOFO 3/25/2026 with ~$1B available. Treat as reopening but politically fragile

name: FEMA Hazard Mitigation Grant Program (HMGP)
entitlement_or_discretionary: Statutory-triggered by Presidential disaster declaration (mandatory funding)
eligible_uses: Post-disaster mitigation tied to a declared disaster; hardening, buyouts, planning
status_2026: STATUTORY and recurring: funded automatically as a percentage of declared-disaster costs; more durable than BRIC because it is not a discretionary competitive line

name: FEMA Flood Mitigation Assistance (FMA)
eligible_uses: Flood mitigation for NFIP-insured properties; buyouts, elevation
status_2026: Recurring; funded from National Flood Insurance Fund; verify FY2026 NOFO

name: FEMA Safeguarding Tomorrow Revolving Loan Fund
eligible_uses: Low-interest loans for hazard mitigation projects
status_2026: Newer RLF model; verify FY2026 capitalization NOFO

name: State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program (SLCGP)
authorizing_statute: Homeland Security Act s.2218; State and Local Cybersecurity Improvement Act (IIJA Div.G)
pass_through_path: FEMA -> State Administrative Agency -> local subrecipient (>=80% pass-through; >=25% to rural)
eligible_uses: Cybersecurity planning, governance, MFA, .gov migration, training
match_cost_share: Increasing local match (10/20/30/40% over the 4 yrs)
status_2026: IN-FLUX: original IIJA authorization expired 9/30/2025; CR extended authorization to 1/30/2026, FY2026 approp extended authorization to 9/30/2026 but provided NO new money for new grants. PILLAR Act (H.R.5078) passed House 11/17/2025 (reauth through FY2035); Senate S.3251 in committee; NOT signed into law as of 6/3/2026. Prior-year/IIJA carryover NOFOs still flowing at state level

name: Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment (BEAD)
eligible_uses: Last-mile broadband deployment, mapping, adoption (priority: unserved/underserved)
status_2026: RESTRUCTURED in 2025 ('Benefit of the Bargain' / tech-neutral round); funds obligated to states but local subgrant timing shifted; verify state office round status

name: Digital Equity Act programs (Planning/Capacity/Competitive)
eligible_uses: Digital inclusion, device access, digital literacy
status_2026: DEAD: $2.75B program terminated 5/9/2025 (Administration deemed it unconstitutional). DO NOT present as live or budget against it

name: USDA ReConnect Program
authorizing_statute: Consolidated Appropriations Act 2018; Farm bill authorities
eligible_uses: Rural broadband construction (loans/grants/combo)
status_2026: Standing RD broadband vehicle; verify FY2026 funding round and rules

name: EDA Public Works & Economic Adjustment Assistance
authorizing_statute: Public Works and Economic Development Act of 1965 (PWEDA)
eligible_uses: Public infrastructure for job creation, economic recovery, planning
status_2026: Standing program; PWEDA reauthorization and annual approp drive levels; verify FY2026 NOFOs and any reorganization

name: Community Development Block Grant (State / Small Cities)
authorizing_statute: Housing and Community Development Act of 1974 Title I
eligible_uses: Infrastructure, housing, public facilities, economic development (>=70% to low/mod income)
status_2026: Long-standing formula block grant; annual appropriation drives level; CDBG admin is an allowable cost line (up to 20% planning+admin cap). Verify FY2026 enacted level

name: USDA Community Facilities Direct/Guaranteed Loans & Grants
authorizing_statute: Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act
eligible_uses: Essential community facilities: public safety, health, municipal buildings
match_cost_share: Grant share by MHI; loans at favorable rates
status_2026: Standing RD program; verify FY2026 allocation

name: USDOT RAISE Discretionary Grants
eligible_uses: Surface transportation capital + planning (road, transit, multimodal)
status_2026: Recurring competitive program; administration priorities shift criteria; verify FY2026 NOFO

name: Safe Streets and Roads for All (SS4A)
eligible_uses: Safety action plans + implementation
status_2026: IIJA funding authorized through FY2026; planning grants are a low-cost entry; verify remaining-year NOFO

name: Energy Efficiency and Conservation Block Grant (EECBG)
authorizing_statute: EISA 2007 Title V subtitle E; IIJA refunded
eligible_uses: Local energy efficiency, building codes, transportation, renewables
status_2026: IIJA refunded EECBG; allocations made to states/locals; verify remaining balances and FY2026 posture under current DOE priorities

name: NHPRC Records & Access Grants
eligible_uses: Preservation, digitization, electronic records, access
status_2026: Small standing grants program; relevant to records-stewardship work; verify FY2026 cycle and any approp change

name: IMLS Grants to States (LSTA)
authorizing_statute: Museum and Library Services Act; LSTA
pass_through_path: IMLS -> state library administrative agency -> local libraries
eligible_uses: Library services, digital access, data, capacity
status_2026: IN-FLUX: IMLS was targeted for reduction by 2025 executive action and litigation; verify FY2026 enacted status before relying on it

name: Community Mental Health Services Block Grant (MHBG)
authorizing_statute: PHS Act Title XIX-B s.1911; 988/crisis set-asides
eligible_uses: Community mental health services for adults/children with SMI/SED; crisis set-aside
status_2026: Recurring formula block grant; supports Rothschild-side behavioral-systems work; verify FY2026 allotment and set-aside percentages

name: Substance Use Prevention, Treatment & Recovery Services Block Grant (SUBG/SABG)
eligible_uses: SUD prevention/treatment/recovery; 20% prevention set-aside
status_2026: Recurring formula block grant; verify FY2026 allotment

name: Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinic (CCBHC)
authorizing_statute: PAMA 2014 s.223; Bipartisan Safer Communities Act (expansion)
entitlement_or_discretionary: Medicaid match is statutory entitlement; SAMHSA grants discretionary
eligible_uses: Comprehensive behavioral health via prospective payment
status_2026: Expanding state Medicaid demonstration model; durable through Medicaid entitlement leg; verify state participation

name: WIOA Adult / Dislocated Worker / Youth
pass_through_path: DOL -> state -> local workforce development board -> provider
eligible_uses: Job training, employment services, sector partnerships
status_2026: Long-standing formula workforce funding; WIOA reauthorization pending but program operates under continuing authority/approp; verify FY2026 allotment

name: Medicaid (Title XIX) administrative match
eligible_uses: Medical assistance + 50% administrative match for systems/admin
status_2026: ENTITLEMENT - the most administration-resilient federal funding leg; 50% admin match can fund eligible systems/compliance work; OBBBA introduced work requirements/cost shifts - verify state-specific impacts

name: ARPA State & Local Fiscal Recovery Funds (SLFRF)
agency: Treasury -> state/local (direct + via state to NEUs)
pass_through_path: Treasury -> recipient (counties/metro cities direct; NEUs via state)
eligible_uses: Broad: govt services, water/sewer/broadband, revenue replacement, public health
period_of_performance: Obligate by 12/31/2024 (passed); EXPEND by 12/31/2026; closeout report 4/30/2027
status_2026: CLOSING: no new obligations allowed (deadline passed 12/31/2024); must fully expend already-obligated funds by 12/31/2026 or return to Treasury; admin/closeout costs eligible. Use to fund Steward work that finishes before 12/31/2026

name: Community Compact IT Grant Program
authorizing_statute: MA Community Compact Cabinet (EO of the Governor)
eligible_uses: One-time IT capital: infrastructure, software, planning/design/training
status_2026: CONFIRMED-LIVE: FY2026 round $200K cap; ~$5M total; applications 1/5/2026-2/5/2026; multi-community/regional joint apps encouraged; not-previously-funded municipalities eligible

name: Community One Stop for Growth (MassWorks / Community Planning / Brownfields)
eligible_uses: Single application portal for MassWorks infrastructure, Community Planning Grant, site readiness, brownfields
status_2026: Standing MA capital portal; Community Planning Grant is a low-cost planning entry; verify annual round dates

name: Municipal Vulnerability Preparedness (MVP)
eligible_uses: Climate resilience planning (MVP Planning) + action grants (MVP Action)
status_2026: Standing MA resilience program; MVP Planning is a low-cost entry that can seed BRIC/HMGP capital; verify annual round

name: Efficiency & Regionalization Grant Program
eligible_uses: Cost-saving regionalization, shared services, efficiency studies
status_2026: Standing MA program supporting regional/shared-service stewardship; verify FY2026 round

name: Municipal technical-assistance vehicles (MTTA / TARPA - client-provided)
eligible_uses: Targeted technical assistance / regional planning support
status_2026: CLIENT-PROVIDED context anchors; specific program figures NOT independently verified this session - confirm exact program names, sponsors, and terms before relying on them

name: Municipal own-source revenue (tax levy / enterprise / capital)
authorizing_statute: Local authority under MA G.L. (e.g., c.44 capital, c.59 levy, Prop 2 1/2)
pass_through_path: Municipal general fund / enterprise fund / capital plan
eligible_uses: Funds the Steward/Continue tail after federal money ends; operating-budget retainer line
status_2026: THE SUSTAINABILITY TEST: own-source absorbs the volatile layer's loss and carries the stewardship cost forward; fully administration-proof
